perturbation.original_text: Buyer shall keep the Confidential Materials confidential and shall not disclose any of the Confidential Materials in any manner whatsoever, provided, however, that (i) Buyer may make any disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Materials to which Owner gives its prior written consent; and (ii) any information contained in the Confidential Materials may be disclosed to Buyer’s Representatives who need to know that information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction with Owner and who agree to keep that information confidential.
perturbation.changed_text: Buyer may keep the Confidential Materials confidential and may disclose any of the Confidential Materials in any manner whatsoever, provided, however, that (i) Buyer may make any disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Materials to which Owner gives its prior written consent; and (ii) any information contained in the Confidential Materials may be disclosed to Buyer’s Representatives who need to know that information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction with Owner and who agree to keep that information confidential.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall not disclose' to 'may disclose' creates a direct contradiction. The initial statement implies an obligation to maintain confidentiality, while the modified statement allows for disclosure. However, this is further in conflict in (i) and (ii), which outlines when the material can be disclosed, making the statement 'may disclose any of the Confidential Materials in any manner whatsoever' highly contradictory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Buyer and its Representatives shall use the Confidential Materials solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible transaction with Owner. Buyer may keep the Confidential Materials confidential and may disclose any of the Confidential Materials in any manner whatsoever, provided, however, that (i) Buyer may make any disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Materials to which Owner gives its prior written consent; and (ii) any information contained in the Confidential Materials may be disclosed to Buyer’s Representatives who need to know that information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction with Owner and who agree to keep that information confidential. Buyer shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'Buyer shall keep the Confidential Materials confidential and shall not disclose any of the Confidential Materials' to 'Buyer may keep the Confidential Materials confidential and may disclose any of the Confidential Materials'. This creates a direct contradiction because the original text mandates confidentiality and prohibits disclosure, while the changed text permits disclosure. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text 'Buyer and its Representatives shall use the Confidential Materials solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible transaction with Owner,' as allowing the buyer to disclose the materials in any manner whatsoever is not consistent with using them solely for evaluation. Furthermore, the modified statement is in conflict with (i) and (ii), which specify under what circumstances the materials can be disclosed, making the statement 'may disclose any of the Confidential Materials in any manner whatsoever' highly contradictory, which is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Buyer agrees that neither Owner nor its Representatives shall have any liability to Buyer or any of its Representatives resulting from Buyer’s use of the Confidential Materials, except as may be expressly set forth in a definitive written agreement, between the parties hereto with respect to a Transaction in accordance with the terms hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Buyer agrees that neither Owner nor its Representatives shall have any liability to Buyer or any of its Representatives resulting from Buyer’s misuse of the Confidential Materials, except as may be expressly set forth in a definitive written agreement, between the parties hereto with respect to a Transaction in accordance with the terms hereof.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'use' to 'misuse' implies that the Owner *is* liable for damages resulting from the proper use of the confidential materials. This creates a contradiction, and is completely opposite of the intention of the contract. It is in conflict with 'Disclaimer of Warranty'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Disclaimer of Warranty. Neither Owner nor its Representatives has made or makes any representation or warranty regarding the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Materials. 3288 Steiner Street, Suite 201, San Francisco, CA 94123 EMAIL: jgates@thekasegroup.com | TEL: 415.231.5231 | FAX: 925.406.0813 | www.thekasegroup.com Buyer agrees that neither Owner nor its Representatives shall have any liability to Buyer or any of its Representatives resulting from Buyer’s misuse of the Confidential Materials, except as may be expressly set forth in a definitive written agreement, between the parties hereto with respect to a Transaction in accordance with the terms hereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Owner is not liable for the Buyer's 'use' of confidential materials. The changed text alters this to 'misuse'. This implies the owner IS liable for use, which directly contradicts the disclaimer in the 'Contradicted Text' stating that the Owner makes no warranty regarding the materials and suggests no liability. This is a clear conflict and inconsistency, aligning with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: As part of Buyer’s agreement to accept and use the Confidential Materials and Audit as such use is defined herein, and as consideration for the release of the Confidential Materials by Owner, BUYER HEREBY WAIVES ANY AND ALL ACTUAL OR POTENTIAL RIGHTS, CLAIMS, CAUSES OF ACTION OR DEMAND BUYER MIGHT HAVE REGARDING ANY FORM OF WARRANTY, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, OF ANY KIND OR TYPE, RELATING TO THE CONFIDENTIAL MATERIALS.
perturbation.changed_text: As part of Buyer’s agreement to accept and hold the Confidential Materials and Audit as such use is defined herein, and as consideration for the release of the Confidential Materials by Owner, BUYER HEREBY WAIVES ANY AND ALL ACTUAL OR POTENTIAL RIGHTS, CLAIMS, CAUSES OF ACTION OR DEMAND BUYER MIGHT HAVE REGARDING ANY FORM OF WARRANTY, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, OF ANY KIND OR TYPE, RELATING TO THE CONFIDENTIAL MATERIALS.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'use' to 'hold' introduces ambiguity. 'Use' implies an active examination for a transaction, while 'hold' suggests mere possession. This makes the agreement unclear. This contradicts section 6, which talks about conducting due diligence
perturbation.contradicted_text: Due Diligence. Buyer acknowledges they will be conducting due diligence with regard to the Property and that such due diligence is the sole responsibility of Buyer. Buyer shall at all times conduct its due diligence review, inspections and examinations in a manner so as to not cause damage, loss, cost or expense to Owner, the Property or the tenants of the Property and so as to not interfere with or disturb any tenant at the Property, and Buyer will indemnify, protect, defend, and hold Owner and the Property harmless from and against any such breach of this Agreement. Without limitation on the foregoing, in no event shall Buyer contact any tenant of the Property without Owner’s express written consent. Owner shall have the right, at its option, to cause a representative of Owner to be present at all inspections, reviews and examinations conducted hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'use' which aligns with conducting 'due diligence'. However, the changed text uses 'hold', implying mere possession without necessarily conducting due diligence. This contradicts the contradicted text, which emphasizes the Buyer's responsibility to conduct due diligence. The shift from 'use' to 'hold' creates an inconsistency regarding the buyer's obligations, creating a contradiction. The Perturbation Explanation mentions that the agreement becomes unclear and contradicts section 6 which talks about conducting due diligence, which is the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Buyer acknowledges that if this Agreement is breached, Owner could not be made whole by monetary damages. Accordingly, Owner, in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled by law or in equity, shall be entitled to an injunction to prevent breaches of this Agreement, and to an order compelling specific performance of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Buyer acknowledges that if this Agreement is breached, Owner could be made whole by monetary damages. Accordingly, Owner, in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled by law or in equity, shall be entitled to an injunction to prevent breaches of this Agreement, and to an order compelling specific performance of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'could not' to 'could' directly contradicts the basis for seeking equitable remedies like an injunction. If monetary damages *are* sufficient, an injunction is less likely to be granted. This contradicts the justification for specific performance, undermining the owner's legal recourse if the buyer breaches. It contradicts with the sentence after, since the sentence after mentions 'injunction' and 'specific performance', which are not likely to occur if the breaches can be made whole by monetary damages.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Remedies. Buyer acknowledges that if this Agreement is breached, Owner could be made whole by monetary damages. Accordingly, Owner, in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled by law or in equity, shall be entitled to an injunction to prevent breaches of this Agreement, and to an order compelling specific performance of this Agreement. Buyer shall reimburse Owner for all costs and expenses, including reasonable attorney’s fees, incurred by Owner if Owner successfully enforces the obligations of Buyer and its Representatives hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters 'could not be made whole by monetary damages' to 'could be made whole by monetary damages'. The contradicted text includes the same language, and the change directly impacts the availability of equitable remedies (injunction, specific performance). By stating that monetary damages *could* be sufficient, it undermines the argument for needing these remedies, creating a direct contradiction within the contract regarding the Owner's recourse in case of a breach. If the Contradicted Text used the original text ('could not'), there would be no contradictions.
